Events

United we stand? Ukraine and the future of the West

Tuesday 10 May 2022Wednesday 20 April 2022 by Harley Richardson

Claire chaired this Academy of Ideas public discussion on 20th April 2022. SPEAKERS: Nick Busvine OBEpartner, Herminius Holdings Ltd; advisory board member and writer, Briefings for Britain; Sevenoaks town councillor; former diplomat, Foreign and Commonwealth Office Professor Bill Durodiéchair of risk and security in international relations, University of Bath Professor Frank Furedisociologist and social commentator; … Read more

Categories Events Tags Academy of Ideas

Ukraine in the crosshairs of history

Tuesday 10 May 2022Monday 14 March 2022 by Harley Richardson

Claire chaired this Academy of Ideas discussion about the conflict in Ukraine, with speakers Frank Furedi, Joan Hoey and Tim Stanley.

Categories Events Tags Academy of Ideas

Claire discusses the Battle of Ideas on GB News

Tuesday 3 January 2023Wednesday 28 July 2021 by Harley Richardson

Claire was a guest on Tonight Live with Dan Wootton on GB News, where she discussed attempts to shut down debate during lockdown, the importance of free and open discussion in a post-pandemic world, and the real-life Battle of Ideas festival event, Open for Debate, taking place in Westminster this Saturday, 31st July. Get your … Read more

Categories Events, Media Tags GB News, Tonight Live with Dan Wootton

The Academy of Ideas – Open for Debate

Wednesday 28 July 2021Tuesday 27 July 2021 by Harley Richardson

John Cronin and Matt Stevens of Reform UK visited the Academy of Ideas office to discuss Open for Debate, a live face-to-face Battle of Ideas festival event taking place in Westminster this coming Saturday 31st July.

Categories Events, Media Tags John Cronin

A year on from Changing Politics for Good

Friday 5 March 2021Saturday 27 February 2021 by Harley Richardson

On 29th February 2020, several hundred democrats came together in a Stockport conference centre to discuss how to build on the radical promise of Brexit. Changing Politics For Good: What Next? was organised by Claire Fox and fellow Brexit Party MEP Henrik Overgaard-Nielsen. While campaigning to defend Brexit they had noticed that the phrase ‘Change … Read more

Categories Events Tags CP4G

Innovations in Democracy: Referenda

Monday 23 May 2022Sunday 18 October 2020 by Harley Richardson

How can referenda can be used to empower people and deepen citizen participation? Claire took part, along with Bruno Kaufmann and Donal Ó Brolcháin, in this discussion organised by the Solonian Democracy Institute. You can hear other discussions from the event here.

Categories Events Tags Solonian Democracy Institute

Institute of Ideas and Arts Live: Left, Right and Covid

Thursday 4 March 2021Monday 27 July 2020 by admin

Isabel Hilton hosts the iai’s online discussion on the changes Covid-19 has brought to the political landscape, from right-wing governments like UK’s Conservative party spending at unprecedented levels to left-wing governments such as Sweden’s Social Democrats refusing to intervene in opposition to the traditionally anti-intervention right. The panellists include Claire Fox, Paul Mason, and Bim … Read more

Categories Events, Media Tags The Institute of Art and Ideas

Ayn Rand Centre UK: Understanding ‘Cancel Culture

Thursday 4 March 2021Saturday 25 July 2020 by admin

The Ayn Rand Centre UK hosts their first online panel event with Razi Ginzberg in conversation with Claire Fox and Craig Biddle. The topic in discussion was the rise of tribalism, toxicity and cancel culture. Collectively do these concepts represent a sign of intolerant times and intellectual shallowness, or are they merely an attempts to … Read more

Categories Events, Media Tags Ayn Rand Centre UK

Live with Littlewood: Sunak, China and Cancel Culture

Thursday 4 March 2021Wednesday 8 July 2020 by admin

Claire joins panellists Isabel Oakshott, and Andrew Lilico among others for the Institute of Economic Affairs’ online event ‘Live with Littlewood’ hosted by IEA Director Mark Littlewood to discuss Rishi Sunak’s fiscal statement, “cancel culture”, and how the West should approach China post-pandemic.

Categories Events, Media Tags Institute of Economic Affairs

CP4G Conference

Friday 5 March 2021Tuesday 31 March 2020 by admin

The Changing Politics for Good #CP4G conference was hosted by former Brexit Party MEPs for the North West, Claire Fox and Henrik Overgaard-Nielsen. It took place in Stockport on 29th February 2020.  The aim of the conference was to discuss the contemporary issues we face, as a nation, in the regions, and in our communities … Read more

‘I STILL Find That Offensive!’ – Book available now

Claire Fox on Twitter
Retweet on Twitter Claire Fox Retweeted
The Free Speech Union @speechunion ·
2 May

As @lara_e_brown writes in @spectator, for a time it seemed we finally had a regulator able to uphold free speech in higher education.

But this week’s judgment marks a major step backwards for free speech on campus. The High Court has effectively endorsed Sussex’s refusal to

Shahrar Ali @shahrarali ·
12h

That's got to be the biggest lie in history. He wants to unlawfully persecute gender critical Greens who would defend the right of women not to have men in their rape crisis centres.

@martingeddes I don't think the government realise how close so many are to being truly homeless if their landlord decides to sell uo while rental stock plummets.
